title: carduus macrocephalus subsp. sporadum
intro: Carduus macrocephalus subsp. sporadum, also known as the Sardinian or Sporadic Large Thistle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic, reduce inflammation, and aid in digestion. The health benefits of Carduus macrocephalus subsp. sporadum include its potential to alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes, improve liver function, and support the cardiovascular system. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for the extraction of its bioactive compounds and utilization of its medicinal properties.
